TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}
TEXT.source: ti sa Qalamudis katua valaɫuvaɫ (Tjakuvukuvuɫ village, p.204)

S.FORM: ka sitsuayan ti sa Qalamudis a Tsuluɫ navaik a qemaɫup.
S.PHON: ka siʦuajan ti sa qaɭamudis a ʦuɭuʎ navaik a qəmaʎup.
S.FORM: ka sicuayan ti sa Qalamudis a Cululj navaik a qemaljup.
S.PHON: ka siʦuajan ti sa qaɣamudis a ʦuɣuʎ navaik a qəmaʎup.
S.TRANSL: Once upon a time Qalamudis of the Tsuluɫ family had been hunting.

S.FORM: manu sekuliɫ, sa kavalut a vuluq.
S.PHON: manu səkuɭiʎ, sa kavaɭut a vuɭuq.
S.FORM: manu sekulilj, sa kavalut a vuluq.
S.PHON: manu səkuɣiʎ, sa kavaɣut a vuɣuq.
S.TRANSL: He tripped and lost hold of his spear,

S.FORM: manu malengleng a sema tjua vatsaɫ.
S.PHON: manu maɭəŋɭəŋ a səma cua vaʦaʎ.
S.FORM: manu malengleng a sema tjua vacalj.
S.PHON: manu maɣəŋɣəŋ a səma ʦua vaʦaʎ.
S.TRANSL: and it slid down into a deep pool.

S.FORM: qau inika keɫang tu kudain nu malap.
S.PHON: qau inika kəʎaŋ tu kudain nu maɭap.
S.FORM: qau inika keljang tu kudain nu malap.
S.PHON: qau inika kəʎaŋ tu kudain nu maɣap.
S.TRANSL: He didn’t know what to do to get it back.

S.FORM: qau ledepen anga a vatsaɫ i Dinualan.
S.PHON: qau ɭədəpən aŋa a vaʦaʎ i dinuaɭan.
S.FORM: qau ledepen anga a vacalj i Dinualan.
S.PHON: qau ɣədəpən aŋa a vaʦaʎ i dinuaɣan.
S.TRANSL: So he dived into the pool at Dinualan.

S.FORM: manu lemdep; a i taladj izua qatjuvi a valaɫuvaɫ.
S.PHON: manu ɭəmdəp; a i taɭaɉ izua qacuvi a vaɭaʎuvaʎ.
S.FORM: manu lemdep; a i taladj izua qatjuvi a valaljuvalj.
S.PHON: manu ɣəmdəp; a i taɣaɉ izua qaʦuvi a vaɣaʎuvaʎ.
S.TRANSL: He dived in; and there in the pool was a monster snake.

S.FORM: "inu anga ku vuluq?" ayain ni sa Qalamudis.
S.PHON: "inu aŋa ku vuɭuq" ajain ni sa qaɭamudis.
S.FORM: "inu anga ku vuluq?" ayain ni sa Qalamudis.
S.PHON: "inu aŋa ku vuɣuq" ajain ni sa qaɣamudis.
S.TRANSL: “Where is my spear?” Qalamudis said to it.

S.FORM: "izua ku siniparasudj tua tjuɫung; alapu," aya a valaɫuvaɫ, qau alapen.
S.PHON: "izua ku siniparasuɉ tua cuʎuŋ; aɭapu," aja a vaɭaʎuvaʎ, qau aɭapən.
S.FORM: "izua ku siniparasudj tua tjuljung; alapu," aya a valaljuvalj, qau alapen.
S.PHON: "izua ku sinipaRasuɉ tua ʦuʎuŋ; aɣapu," aja a vaɣaʎuvaʎ, qau aɣapən.
S.TRANSL: “I lined it up there with the bamboo tube; take it,” said the monster. So he took it.

S.FORM: maɫavar tiamadju: "parepaqali-i; vava-i sa tja kinvava," aya a maɫavar.
S.PHON: maʎavar tiamaɉu: "parəpaqaɭi-i; vava-i sa ca kinvava," aja a maʎavar.
S.FORM: maljavar tiamadju: "parepaqali-i; vava-i sa tja kinvava," aya a maljavar.
S.PHON: maʎavaR tiamaɉu: "paRəpaqaɣi-i; vava-i sa ʦa kinvava," aja a maʎavaR.
S.TRANSL: They said to one another: “Let’s become friends. Let’s make beer and drink it,” they said to one another.

S.FORM: qau mirava ti sa Qalamudis, kemuɫup, kematsing, qemezetj tua pinuɫayɫayan; sa vaik a ɫetaladj tua zaɫum a kinvava.
S.PHON: qau mirava ti sa qaɭamudis, kəmuʎup, kəmaʦiŋ, qəməzəc tua pinuʎajʎajan; sa vaik a ʎətaɭaɉ tua zaʎum a kinvava.
S.FORM: qau mirava ti sa Qalamudis, kemuljup, kemacing, qemezetj tua pinuljayljayan; sa vaik a ljetaladj tua zaljum a kinvava.
S.PHON: qau miRava ti sa qaɣamudis, kəmuʎup, kəmaʦiŋ, qəməzəʦ tua pinuʎajʎajan; sa vaik a ʎətaɣaɉ tua zaʎum a kinvava.
S.TRANSL: Qalamudis got ready; he put on a sleeveless jacket and leggings, and pressed on his head an eagle-feather decoration; and he went into the water for a drinking party.

S.FORM: qau mapulaw anga ti sa Qalamudis; sema katsauan.
S.PHON: qau mapuɭaw aŋa ti sa qaɭamudis; səma kaʦauan.
S.FORM: qau mapulaw anga ti sa Qalamudis; sema kacauan.
S.PHON: qau mapuɣaw aŋa ti sa qaɣamudis; səma kaʦauan.
S.TRANSL: Qalamudis got drunk. As he came out

S.FORM: "pangtjezu anga i tjanuaken nu tsuatsuay anga," ayain ni sa Qalamudis.
S.PHON: "paŋcəzu aŋa i canuakən nu ʦuaʦuaj aŋa," ajain ni sa qaɭamudis.
S.FORM: "pangtjezu anga i tjanuaken nu cuacuay anga," ayain ni sa Qalamudis.
S.PHON: "paŋʦəzu aŋa i ʦanuakən nu ʦuaʦuaj aŋa," ajain ni sa qaɣamudis.
S.TRANSL: he said: “After a time you come to my place,” he said to the monster.

S.FORM: qau mangtjez a valaɫuvaɫ i tjai Tsuluɫ a kinvava.
S.PHON: qau maŋcəz a vaɭaʎuvaʎ i cai ʦuɭuʎ a kinvava.
S.FORM: qau mangtjez a valaljuvalj i tjai Cululj a kinvava.
S.PHON: qau maŋʦəz a vaɣaʎuvaʎ i ʦai ʦuɣuʎ a kinvava.
S.TRANSL: So the monster came to the Tsuluɫ house for a drinking party.

S.FORM: sa mapulaw anga azua valaɫuvaɫ; vaik anga tsemikel.
S.PHON: sa mapuɭaw aŋa azua vaɭaʎuvaʎ; vaik aŋa ʦəmikəɭ.
S.FORM: sa mapulaw anga azua valaljuvalj; vaik anga cemikel.
S.PHON: sa mapuɣaw aŋa azua vaɣaʎuvaʎ; vaik aŋa ʦəmikəɣ.
S.TRANSL: The monster got drunk, and then went back.

S.FORM: maruquruqu i djalan, aya.
S.PHON: maruquruqu i ɉaɭan, aja.
S.FORM: maruquruqu i djalan, aya.
S.PHON: maRuquRuqu i ɉaɣan, aja.
S.TRANSL: It rolled about in the path, they say.

S.FORM: sa azazua qali ni sa Qalamudis, nu kiqenetj a qaliqali, qatjuvi; ɫakua nu kiqenetj ti sa Qalamudis, tsautsau, aya a tjautsikel.
S.PHON: sa azazua qaɭi ni sa qaɭamudis, nu kiqənəc a qaɭiqaɭi, qacuvi; ʎakua nu kiqənəc ti sa qaɭamudis, ʦauʦau, aja a cauʦikəɭ.
S.FORM: sa azazua qali ni sa Qalamudis, nu kiqenetj a qaliqali, qatjuvi; ljakua nu kiqenetj ti sa Qalamudis, caucau, aya a tjaucikel.
S.PHON: sa azazua qaɣi ni sa qaɣamudis, nu kiqənəʦ a qaɣiqaɣi, qaʦuvi; ʎakua nu kiqənəʦ ti sa qaɣamudis, ʦauʦau, aja a ʦauʦikəɣ.
S.TRANSL: Now that friend of Qalamudis, when others looked at it, it was a snake; but when Qalamudis looked at it, it was human, according to the story.
